notes_for_human_reviewer: DIMENSIONAL INVERSION — STRONG MITIGATION, NO NAMED OWNERS. Mitigations are crisp, specific, dated, with dollar amounts and backup plans. Risk identification is solid. But every single owner is 'Project team' / 'Eng team' / 'IT Ops' — no named individuals. The rubric should score ownership lower for team-level vs. named individual accountability. CALIBRATION NOTE (2026-05-04): first run scored ownership at 3 — Claude treats role-level ownership ('IT Ops', 'Eng team') as acceptable when paired with strong mitigations. Defensible since 'IT Ops' is at least a specific function vs. 'project team'. Widened to score=2 ±2 (allows 0-4), keeping the dim below 'fully named' ceiling. Tests whether grader correctly isolates ownership from mitigation realism — common drift is full halo to 5.
